function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
Is there a call path from function 'main' to function 'printf'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 's<=n/2+1' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 's<=n/2+1' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'j<n-s-1' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'j<n-s-1' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 7:  <statement> int arr[N][N]={0}; </statement> is reached, the statement line 43:  <statement> printf("    "); </statement> has already been reached?
Is it guaranteed that whenever line 34:  <statement> for(i=0;i<N;i++) </statement> is reached, the statement line 12:  <statement> for(j;j<n-s-1;j++) </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable j at line 22: <statement> 	for(j;j>s;j--) </statement> depend on the value of variable j at line 12: <statement> 	for(j;j<n-s-1;j++) </statement>?
Does variable arr at line 7: <statement> 	int arr[N][N]={0}; </statement> determine the value stored in variable s at line 10: <statement> 	for(s=0;s<=n/2+1;s++) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'i' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'count' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'arr[i][j]=count++;' be considered dead code?
